verdicts.note: The dossier lists 'increase access to behavioral health care' under Health care / civil rights [S1], not as a stated public-safety position. Reading it as her public-safety frame is an indirect inference; the score's basis ('frames public safety around...') overstates what S1 says. Direction is plausible but this is low confidence, not medium.

Score and confidence are airtight (9+ years, passed legislation, committee leadership per S1/S3). Flag: the candidate highlight cites the Laurie Jinkins endorsement to [S1], but per the dossier the Speaker endorsement appears on the campaign site [S2]; the pamphlet [S1] lists only organizations.

Rests on a single pamphlet phrase ('increase access to behavioral health care' [S1]) plus an argument from silence (no universal-coverage proposal). The phrase states no position on system structure; 'within existing structures' is the scorer's gloss, not the source's. Indirect inference = low confidence.

+2 (fully pole_b) is exaggerated. Evidence is one generic pamphlet pledge to 'protect civil rights' plus endorsements [S1]. Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates supports the direction; Alliance for Gun Responsibility is not a social-axis signal under this rubric (reproductive rights, LGBTQ+, curricula). No explicit statements on reproductive or gender-affirming care or schools; endorsement pattern supports +1 medium, not the maximal score.

+1 cherry-picks one narrow rule (closed-loop cooling for data centers [S2]) while the same source shows him prioritizing AI/robotics career pathways — pole_a's 'treat tech/AI growth as opportunity.' The score's own basis concedes the tension. Genuinely mixed evidence belongs at 0; keep low confidence.

The sole cited evidence is 'supports legal immigration' [S1], which is direction-neutral: pole_b's own description includes 'legal pathways.' The scorer's basis admits it is an 'indirect signal' with 'no detailed position given.' Reading -1 into this rests on party affiliation, not dossier evidence — a score smuggled from party preference. No scoreable position exists.
